question.text: As per the SEBI circular of October 2024 (effective November 20, 2024), the key restriction on weekly index options is:

answer.text: Each exchange can offer weekly expiry derivatives contracts for only one of its benchmark indices
Weekly options are completely banned on all exchanges
Each exchange can offer weekly options on up to five indices
Weekly options are limited to stock options only, not index options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's October 2024 circular limits weekly index options to ONE benchmark index per exchange. This reduces the proliferation of weekly expiry products that contributed to speculative activity. NSE can offer weekly options on Nifty OR Bank Nifty (not both). **The Trap:** Weekly options are not banned — they're restricted to one benchmark index per exchange. The restriction is on the NUMBER of indices with weekly options, not on weekly options per se. **Cross-Exam Context:** Weekly options rationalization is a frequently tested recent SEBI measure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 1 benchmark weekly index per exchange. Effective Nov 20, 2024. Other indices = monthly only.
